english: W. fist, *mul-túg ćò-će, gám-će* (*mul thug co ce, gam ce*) to threaten with the fist, *gyáb-će* (*rgyab ce*) to strike with the fist.

I. num. fig.: 106. — II. sbst. 1. resp. žugs (zhugs) C., *náṅ-me* (*nang me*) W., fire, me (me) bar (bar) the fire burns, šor (shor) breaks out, mčed (mched) spreads, ši (shi) is extinguished; *me sóṅ-na* (*me song na*) W. is the fire burning (again)? kȟáṅ-pa (khang pa) mes (mes) (vulgo *mé-la* (*me la*)) bsregs (bsregs), šor (shor), kyer (khyer) the house is burnt down, *dugs soṅ* (*dugs song*) W. ignited, burnt (partially); me sbór-ba (me sbor ba), búd-pa ('bud pa), gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) B., *(s)bár-će, ṗú-će, dúg-će* (*sbar ce, phu ce, dug ce*) W. to light a fire, gsó-ba (gso ba), *sóṅ-te ćò-će* (*song te co ce*) W. to stir, poke, trim the fire, *nyál-će* (*nyal ce*) W. to cover the glowing embers with ashes, in order to preserve the heat; rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa) 1. to set on fire, kyi̇́m-la (khyim la) a house Glr. 2. to strike fire W., me ldé-ba (me lde ba) B. and col., to warm one's self at the fire. — 2. symb. num.: 3. — Comp. me-skám (me skam) cock (of a gun), *me-kám ǰar tsar* (*me kam jar tsar*) W. the gun is cocked. — me-skyógs (me skyogs) C. a shovel for live coals. — me-sgyógs (me sgyogs), gyogs (gyogs) = sgyogs (sgyogs) 2. — me-mgál (me mgal) fire-brand, me-mgál-gyi kór-lo (me mgal gyi skor lo) the circle made by a firebrand, when quickly swung round Cs. — *me-dóṅ* (*me dong*) torch C. — mé-čan (me can) fiery, containing fire. — me-lčágs (me lcags) fire-steel, pocket-fire. — mé-lče (me lce) flame of fire. — me-čá (me cha) fire-steel (?) Sch., *me - čé* (*me chas*) C. every thing requisite for kindling a fire, as it is got in readiness for the following morning. — me-mnyam-rlúṅ (me mnyam rlung) v. rluṅ (rlung). — *me-tág* (*me ltag*) C. 1. (rtags) (rtags) a mark of burning. 2. (ltag (ltag) or stag (stag)) spark, sparklet, a bit of live coal in the ashes. — me-táb (me thab) fire-place, hearth; stove. — me-dóṅ (me dong) Dzl. fire-pit, pool of fire. — me-dród (me drod) v. drod (drod). — *me-dá* (*me mda'*) C., musket, pistol; *me-da pag-čéṅ* (*me mda' pag ceng*) canon Schr.; *me-dá gyáb-pa* (*me mda' rgyab pa*) to discharge a gun; *me-da-ši̇́ṅ* (*me mda' shing*) resinous wood, the coal of which is particularly used for making gun-powder. — *me-dág* (*me mdag*) (mdag) (mdag) C. coals glowing underneath the ashes. — me-rdél (me rdel) bullet, musket-ball Sch. — me-rdó (me rdo) flint Cs. — me-núr (me nur) Sch. = me-mdág (me mdag). — me-snód (me snod), or -pór (-por) coal-pan, chafing-dish, perfuming-pan. — me-púṅ (me phung), me-búm (me bum) cupping-glass, cup Lt. — mé-ba (me ba) Dzl. = me (me). — mé-bo (me bo) = me (me) a large fire, mé-bo čé (me bo che) Dzl. — me-dbál (me dbal) a disease Med.; it is said to be a cutaneous eruption, hot and smarting, perh. erysipelas? — me-múr (me mur) = me-mdág (me mdag) Dzl.; me-ma-múr (me ma mur) Thgy. id.? — me-btsá (me btsa') v. btsa (btsa'). — *me-tság* (*me tshag*) spark W. — *me-dzé* (*me rdzas*) gun-powder C. — me-γźi (me gzhi) anvil Sch. — me-γżól (me gzhol) mark of singeing, of having caught fire. — *me-zí* (*me zi*) W. = me-ltág (me ltag). — me-̨óbs (me 'obs) = me-dóṅ (me dong) Sch. — mé-ri (me ri) fire-mountain, introduced by us for volcano. — me-rís (me ris) a figure resembling a flame Sch. — me-ró (me ro) an extinguished fire, fig. bstán-pai me-ró laṅ (bstan pa'i me ro lang) Glr. the extinct doctrine revives again. — *me-li̇́ṅ* (*me ling*) W. flame. — me-léṅ (me leng) fire-tongs. — me-šél (me shel) burning-glass. — me-lhá (me lha) the god of fire, v. Schl. 251 sqq. — III. v. also mé-tog (me tog).

W. *mén-tog* (*men tog*), 1. flower, mé-tog bar (me tog bar), kȟa ʽbus (kha 'bus) the flower opens, begins to bloom, mé-tog-gi ṗréṅ-ba (me tog gi phreng ba) chaplet, wreath of flowers. — 2. W. tuft or crest on the head of some birds. — 3. W. snow-flake.

1. mirror, looking-glass, frq.; lás-kyi mé-loṅ (las kyi me long) a magic mirror, revealing the future Glr.; also fig., esp. in titles of books, e.g. rgyal-rábs-kyi gsál-bai mé-loṅ (rgyal rabs kyi gsal ba'i me long) A bright Mirror of the History of Kings. 2. plain surface, flat body extending in length and breadth, e.g. the flatness of the shoulder-blade, table-top, door-pannels etc., hence sgo mé-loṅ-čan (sgo me long can) Glr. an opening provided with a frame of boards to close it, not merely an 'ostium', of which description most of the inner doors in Tibetan houses are.

for mi yód-pa (mi yod pa) to be not, to exist not (v. yód-pa (yod pa)), med (med) he is not here, he is gone etc.; *kȟa-čúl-du soṅ-te med* (*kha cul du song te med*) W. he is off, having gone to Kashmere; *čag-mag a-pẹ kyér-te med* (*lcag mag a pas khyer te med*) W. the tinder-box is not here, father has taken it with him; *ši-te med* (*shi te med*) W. he is dead and gone; skabs med (skabs med) Dzl. there is, or there was, no opportunity; čos-kyi miṅ tsam yaṅ med (chos kyi ming tsam yang med) Glr. religious law does not, or did not, exist at all; med kyaṅ (med kyang) even if nothing is extant,
